children.name: ValidateCommand
children.purpose: Check that the command is one of the allowed four and that required fields in message_data are present
children.behavior: If command is not in ['send','history','create_channel','join'], return error. For each command, verify necessary fields exist in message_data.
children.signature: def ValidateCommand(command: str, message_data: dict) -> dict

constraints.description: Must not modify any data store

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error for missing required fields

children.stop_reason: Pure validation logic, no further decomposition needed

children.name: SendMessage
children.purpose: Execute the 'send' command: validate membership, store message, update last_seen, notify members
children.behavior: Check user is member of channel via channels store. If not, return error. Store message in messages store with timestamp. Update user's last_seen in users store. Retrieve channel members and prepare notification data (message details).
children.signature: def SendMessage(message_data: dict, channels: Database, messages: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Check membership and get member list

data_operations.description: Check user membership and retrieve member list

constraints.description: Must verify user is member of channel before storing message
Must update last_seen atomically with message storage

acceptance_criteria.description: Message stored with correct channel_id, user_id, content, timestamp
User's last_seen updated to current timestamp
Returns error if user not in channel

children.name: GetHistory
children.purpose: Execute the 'history' command: retrieve last 100 messages from a channel sorted by timestamp descending
children.behavior: Query messages store for all messages with given channel_id, sort by timestamp descending, limit to 100, return list.
children.signature: def GetHistory(message_data: dict, messages: Database) -> dict

data_operations.description: Retrieve messages filtered by channel_id, sorted by timestamp descending, limited to 100

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns list of messages with correct channel_id
Returns empty list if no messages in channel

children.stop_reason: Simple query operation, no further decomposition needed

children.name: CreateChannel
children.purpose: Execute the 'create_channel' command: create new channel with unique name, set creator as first member
children.behavior: Check if channel name already exists in channels store. If yes, return error. Generate new channel_id, store channel with name, members=[user_id], created_by=user_id.
children.signature: def CreateChannel(message_data: dict, channels: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Check name uniqueness and store new channel

data_operations.description: Check if channel name already exists
Store new channel with name, members, created_by

constraints.description: Must ensure channel name is unique
Must set creator as first member

acceptance_criteria.description: New channel created with unique name
Returns error if channel name already exists

children.stop_reason: Simple create operation, no further decomposition needed

children.name: JoinChannel
children.purpose: Execute the 'join' command: add user to channel member list if not already a member
children.behavior: Check if user_id is already in channel's members list. If yes, return error. Otherwise, append user_id to members list and update channels store.
children.signature: def JoinChannel(message_data: dict, channels: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Check membership and update member list

data_operations.description: Check if user is already a member

constraints.description: Must verify channel exists before adding member

acceptance_criteria.description: User added to channel members list
Returns error if user already member
Returns error if channel does not exist

children.stop_reason: Simple join operation, no further decomposition needed

governance_notes: Parent global_vars conservation verified: messages (read_write) covered by SendMessage (write) and GetHistory (read); users (read) covered by SendMessage (write) for last_seen update; channels (read_write) covered by SendMessage (read), CreateChannel (read_write), JoinChannel (read_write). No parent operation dropped.
